rulings.Case Title: HOME VEST CAPITAL LLC VS. CHINA ELLIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Response To Post-Judgment Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 13, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF HOME VEST CAPITAL LLC's Motion To Compel Response To Post-Judgment Interrogatories. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is continued to January 30, 2015, to give the moving party a chance to comply with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. The moving party shall immediately provide courtesy copies of its papers with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to klgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DIANA D. SAM VS. CATALINA BOYKIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents, Responses To Interrogatories, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 13, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANT CATALINA BOYKIN's Motion To Compel Production Of Documents, Responses To Interrogatories, And For Sanctions. (Part 1 of 2.) Pro Tem Judge Katherine Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The court did not receive a courtesy copy of plaintiff's opposition. Plaintiff to bring copy to the hearing and the court will consider it. Defendants' motion to compel further responses and production of documents is as follows: All objections are overruled. The court does not find any of the requests to be overly broad or vague. Plaintiff's objection on the grounds of right of privacy is overruled. Personal financial information comes within the zone of privacy protected by Article I, § 1 of the California Constitution. Valley Bank of Nevada v. Superior Court (1975) 15 Cal. 3d 652, 656 Tax records are also specifically protected by statute and case law. California Revenue and Tax Code §19542 et seq.; Webb v. Standard Oil Co. (1957) 49 Cal. 2d. 509 When an individual's right of privacy in his financial affairs conflicts with the public need for discovery in litigation, the competing interests must be carefully balanced. Moskowitz v. Superior Court 137 C.A. 3d 313,316 citing Valley Bank of Nevada v. Superior Court, supra. The present lawsuit involves a financial transaction between the parties. It is defendants' position that plaintiff did not have the monies to fund the escrow account. This makes the discovery of the limited financial records discovery. Furthermore, plaintiff brought her her financials at the time of the close of escrow and her other real estate transactions into issue when she filed the present lawsuit and thus has waived her privacy rights. Plaintiff to serve a verified response and produce documents to this document request no later than January 26, 2015. (Continued to Part 2.) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: HECTOR PAZOS VS. CASA THAI MARKET, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Compel Further Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions Or In The Alternative Request For Terminating Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 13, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANTs CASA THAI MARKET, INC. and HUNG LI's Motion Compel Further Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions Or In The Alternative Request For Terminating Sanctions. (Part 1 of 2) Pro Tem Judge Katherine Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' motion to compel further responses to discovery is unopposed and is granted as follows: The court finds that Plaintiff failed to make a good faith effort to respond to Form Interrogatories #9.1, 9.2, 12.1, 12.2, 12.3, 12.4, 12.5, 12.7, 14.1 and 14.2 and as well as comply with the Court Order of October 24, 2014. The code requires that a party must make a reasonable and good faith effort to obtain the information. Regency Health Services, Inc. v. Superior Court (1998) 64 CA4th 1496"A party cannot plead ignorance to information which can be obtained from sources under his control." Deyo v. Kilbourne (1978) 84 CA3d 771,782. This includes a party's lawyer Smith v. Sup. Ct (Alfred) (1961) 189 CA 2d 6, agents or employees Gordon v. Sup. Ct. (U.Z.MFG.Co) (1984) 161 CA 3d 15,167-168, family members Jones v. Superior Court (Benny) (1981) 119 CA 3d 534, 552 and experts who have been retained by a party and designated as a trial witness. Sigerseth v. Superior Court(1972) 23 CA 3d 427,433. See Weil and Brown, Cal Prac. Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2009) 8:1051-1060 This means that you can't just pawn off the responses to your client or spend an hour and dictate off the top of your head and then answer "inability to respond." See Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting, Inc. v. Pacific Healthcare Consultants (2007) 148 CA4th 390 The statute and the case law make it very clear that a party and the attorney must be proactive in obtaining the information to respond to the interrogatories. Plaintiff to serve further verified responses to the interrogatories no later than January 26, 2015. (Continued to Part 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JORDANNE HO-SHUE et al VS. GDL SFO, INC. DBA GOLD DUST LOUNGE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 13, 2015, Line 8, DEFENDANT GDL SFO, INC., DBA GOLD DUST AT THE WHARF DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled. As to the first cause of action, the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") adequately alleges that Plaintiff worked overtime. (FAC, par. 17.) As to the third cause of action, the FAC adequately alleges that Plaintiff was deprived of the opportunity to take meal and rest breaks. (FAC, pars. 9, 14, 18.) As to the fourth cause of action, the FAC adequately alleges that Plaintiff was owed wages at the time of her termination that were improperly withheld. (FAC, par. 19.) As to the second and the fifth causes of action, because the FAC adequately alleges Labor Code violations, the FAC also adequately alleges violation of Bus. & Prof. Code sec. 17200. As to the class action allegations, "class suitability should not be determined on demurrer." (Prince v. CLS Transp., Inc. (2004) 118 Cal.App.4th 1320, 1325.) In Prince, the Court noted that a determination of class suitability is not appropriate at the pleadings stage "particularly" in those cases "involving wage and hour claims." (Id.) All that is required to survive a demurrer challenging class suitability are allegations that tend to show: "(1) an ascertainable class of plaintiffs, and (2) questions of law and fact which are common to the class." (Id. at 1326.) The FAC pleads facts showing these two elements. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: GRAHAM SCHNEIDER VS. ANN SACKS TILE & STONE INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 13, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANT ANN SACKS TILE & STONE INC., JEFF FOUNTAIN DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The Demurrer is overruled in part and sustained in part. The As to the fraud cause of action, the Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend to permit Plaintiff the opportunity to meet the heightened pleading requirements for fraud causes of action. (See Lazar v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 49 Cal.4th 631, 645 [In asserting a fraud claim against a corporate employer, a plaintiff must "allege the names of the persons who made the allegedly fraudulent representations, their authority to speak, to whom they spoke, what they said or wrote, and when it was said or written."].) As to the cause of action for unfair competition, the Demurrer is overruled, as Plaintiff sufficiently alleges a claim under the unfairness prong, which is all that is required. (See In re Toyota Motor Corp. Unintended Acceleration Marketing (C.D. Cal. 2010) 754 F.Supp.2d 1145, 1175 [a plaintiff may pursue a UCL claim under any or all three theories].) Finally, as to the breach of written contract claim, the Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend for Plaintiff to adequately allege the terms of the contract or the legal intendment and effect of the contract. (See Construction Protective Services, Inc. v. TIG Specialty Ins. Co. (2002) 29 Cal.4th 189, 198-199.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: GRAHAM SCHNEIDER VS. ANN SACKS TILE & STONE INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 13, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANT ANN SACKS TILE & STONE INC., JEFF FOUNTAIN MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The Motion is granted with 10 days leave to amend. The Complaint fails to state a cause of action for fraud (see tentative ruling on the Demurrer), and no other allegations in the Complaint are sufficient to support a claim for punitive damages.
